High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall implement quality checking processes as soon as data become available.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide the capability for administrators to configure the set of quality checking processes to be invoked upon arrival of new environmental data.
CAS shall maintain configuration parameters that define the triggering conditions under which Quality Checking Services are notified of newly available data.
CAS shall allow administrators to enable or disable specific quality checking processes on a per-data-source basis.
CAS shall provide an interface for administrators to define and update the priority order in which quality checking processes are executed as data becomes available.
CAS shall store and manage configuration settings specifying the maximum allowable latency between data availability and initiation of quality checking processes.
CAS shall validate administrator-supplied configuration changes related to quality checking triggers prior to applying them to the system.
CAS shall log all configuration changes affecting the initiation of quality checking processes, including timestamp and responsible administrator identity.
CAS shall provide the capability to notify designated system components when configuration changes affecting quality checking initiation have been applied.
CAS shall support rollback of quality checking configuration settings to a previous valid state in the event of an erroneous update.
CAS shall provide administrators the ability to configure retry policies for quality checking process initiation in the event of an initial failure.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall implement quality checking processes as soon as data become available.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall monitor for the availability of newly collected or entered environmental data and initiate quality checking processes immediately upon detection.
The Quality Checking Services shall subscribe to data availability notifications from upstream collection and entry components to trigger quality checking without manual intervention.
The Quality Checking Services shall process incoming data records in the order received to ensure timely quality checking of all available data.
The Quality Checking Services shall apply the applicable set of quality checks to each data element based on its data type and source.
The Quality Checking Services shall complete quality checking of a data record within a configurable maximum processing time from the moment the data becomes available.
The Quality Checking Services shall assign a quality flag to each data element indicating the outcome of the quality checking process.
The Quality Checking Services shall log the date and time at which quality checking begins and completes for each data record.
The Quality Checking Services shall support concurrent quality checking of multiple data streams to minimize latency when data from multiple sources become available simultaneously.
The Quality Checking Services shall retrieve reference and threshold values required for quality checks from the appropriate metadata or configuration sources prior to processing.
The Quality Checking Services shall forward quality-checked data, along with associated quality flags, to the appropriate downstream service immediately upon completion of checking.
The Quality Checking Services shall detect and handle late-arriving or out-of-sequence data by initiating quality checking as soon as such data is received.
The Quality Checking Services shall generate an alert or notification if quality checking cannot be initiated within a specified time after data becomes available.
The Quality Checking Services shall be capable of scaling processing resources to maintain near real-time quality checking performance during periods of high data throughput.
The Quality Checking Services shall re-evaluate previously checked data if updated reference values or configuration changes affecting quality checks become available.
The Quality Checking Services shall record and report any failures or exceptions encountered during the quality checking process for subsequent review.

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Schedule Service shall detect the arrival of new data and trigger a corresponding quality checking task without manual intervention.
The Schedule Service shall subscribe to data availability notifications from Collector Services and Manual Entry User Interface sources.
The Schedule Service shall queue quality checking jobs for execution immediately upon receipt of a data availability event.
The Schedule Service shall initiate the Quality Checking Services process within a configurable maximum delay after data becomes available.
The Schedule Service shall support configuration of scheduling rules that define which incoming data types trigger quality checking processes.
The Schedule Service shall prioritize scheduling of quality checking tasks based on configurable priority levels associated with data sources.
The Schedule Service shall log the timestamp of data availability and the timestamp of quality checking task initiation for audit purposes.
The Schedule Service shall retry scheduling of a quality checking task if the initial triggering attempt fails.
The Schedule Service shall notify the Watchdog subsystem if a quality checking task fails to be scheduled within the configured time threshold.
The Schedule Service shall support concurrent scheduling of multiple quality checking tasks for simultaneously arriving data sets.
The Schedule Service shall allow administrators to configure the maximum allowable latency between data availability and quality checking initiation.
The Schedule Service shall provide status information on pending, in-progress, and completed quality checking scheduling requests to the Configuration & Administration Service.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall record the methods applied when deriving quality checking information.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide a mechanism to configure and store the set of quality checking methods available for application to environmental data.
CAS shall maintain a persistent record of each quality checking method applied, including method identifier, version, and configuration parameters used.
CAS shall associate each recorded quality checking method with the specific data elements or datasets to which it was applied.
CAS shall record the date and time at which each quality checking method was applied.
CAS shall record the identity of the system component or user that invoked each quality checking method.
CAS shall provide administrators with the ability to view, query, and export the history of quality checking methods applied to a given dataset.
CAS shall support configuration of retention policies for quality checking method records to ensure historical traceability.
CAS shall provide an interface for authorized users to update or version quality checking method definitions while preserving records of prior definitions.
CAS shall ensure that records of applied quality checking methods are immutable once created, except through authorized versioning processes.
CAS shall provide validation to ensure that all quality checking methods applied are recorded before associated quality checking results are finalized.
CAS shall log any changes to the configuration of quality checking methods, including who made the change and when.
CAS shall provide an audit trail linking recorded quality checking methods to the derived quality checking information they produced.

CAS shall provide the ability to define scheduling or effective date ranges for quality checking rules.

The QChS shall allow situation-specific quality checking rules to override or supplement general (non-situation-specific) quality checking rules when both are applicable to the same data element.
The QChS shall support prioritization or sequencing of situation-specific rules when multiple environmental situations are detected simultaneously for the same observation.
The QChS shall log which situation-specific rule(s) were applied to each observation, including the identified environmental situation that triggered the rule.
The QChS shall provide an interface allowing administrators to associate specific quality checking rules with defined environmental situation types via CAS/CAUI.
The QChS shall validate that situation-specific rule definitions include the required parameters, thresholds, and applicable environmental condition criteria before activation.
The QChS shall re-evaluate previously processed observations using updated situation-specific rules when such rules are modified, if reprocessing is requested.
The QChS shall handle cases where no environmental situation is detected by applying only the default (non-situational) quality checking rules.
The QChS shall report an error or warning when a defined situation-specific rule references an environmental situation type that is not recognized or available.
The QChS shall support enabling and disabling individual situation-specific quality checking rules without affecting other configured rules.
The QChS shall provide the results of situation-specific quality checks, including the associated environmental situation identifier, to QEDS for further processing.

The Quality Checking Services shall compute statistical baselines (e.g., mean, standard deviation, min/max) from historical environmental data for each monitored parameter and site.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to use multiple algorithms for its quality checking process.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide the capability to register, store, and manage configuration records for multiple quality checking algorithms.
CAS shall allow an administrator to define which quality checking algorithm(s) are active for a given data type, sensor, or observation type.
CAS shall maintain configuration parameters specific to each quality checking algorithm, including algorithm-specific thresholds and settings.
CAS shall provide a mechanism to enable or disable individual quality checking algorithms without affecting the operation of other configured algorithms.
CAS shall support versioning of quality checking algorithm configurations to track changes over time.
CAS shall validate quality checking algorithm configuration data for completeness and correctness prior to committing changes.
CAS shall provide the capability to assign a priority or execution order among multiple configured quality checking algorithms.
CAS shall maintain an audit trail of all changes made to quality checking algorithm configurations, including the identity of the user making the change and the timestamp.
CAS shall provide configuration data for quality checking algorithms to other subsystems upon request.
CAS shall support the addition of new quality checking algorithm configurations without requiring modification to existing algorithm configurations.
CAS shall provide the capability to associate multiple quality checking algorithms with a single monitored parameter or station.
CAS shall restrict modification of quality checking algorithm configurations to users with appropriate administrative privileges.
CAS shall provide a means to retrieve the current and historical configuration state of each quality checking algorithm on demand.
CAS shall notify subscribing subsystems of changes to quality checking algorithm configurations.

The EMC shall log all create, update, and delete operations performed on metadata records.
The EMC shall provide a mechanism to synchronize metadata updates received from Configuration & Administration Service.
The EMC shall maintain metadata integrity constraints to prevent duplicate entries for the same environmental data source.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall allow access to new surface transportation-related environmental data.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall perform quality checks on new surface transportation-related environmental data received from the Collector Services before it is made available for access.

The QChS shall apply configurable quality check algorithms specific to surface transportation-related environmental data types, including but not limited to pavement condition, visibility, and road weather sensor data.
The QChS shall assign a quality flag to each new surface transportation-related environmental data element indicating the result of the quality checking process.
The QChS shall detect and flag out-of-range values in newly received surface transportation-related environmental data based on configurable thresholds.
The QChS shall detect and flag spatial inconsistencies in surface transportation-related environmental data by comparing values against neighboring observation stations.
The QChS shall detect and flag temporal inconsistencies in surface transportation-related environmental data by comparing current values against historical trends from the same station.
The QChS shall detect and flag data from surface transportation sensors that report duplicate or stale (non-updating) values over a configurable time period.

The QChS shall support the addition of new quality check algorithms to accommodate newly introduced surface transportation-related environmental data types without requiring redesign of existing checks.
The QChS shall log the results of all quality checks performed on new surface transportation-related environmental data for auditing and traceability purposes.

The QChS shall forward quality-checked surface transportation-related environmental data, along with associated quality flags, to the Qualified Environmental Data Services for access by downstream consumers.
The QChS shall notify the Configuration & Administration Service when a new surface transportation-related environmental data type is received that lacks a defined quality check configuration.
The QChS shall complete quality checking of new surface transportation-related environmental data within a configurable maximum processing time to support timely data access.

The QChS shall allow administrators to configure quality check parameters and thresholds for each new surface transportation-related environmental data type via the Configuration & Administration Service.
The QChS shall reject or quarantine surface transportation-related environmental data that fails critical quality checks, preventing it from being accessed until reviewed.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall enable administrators to manage security groups.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide functionality to create new security groups with a unique group identifier and descriptive name.
CAS shall provide functionality to modify existing security group attributes, including name, description, and status.
CAS shall provide functionality to delete security groups that are not currently assigned to any active administrator accounts.
CAS shall allow administrators to assign one or more security groups to a given user account.
CAS shall allow administrators to remove security group assignments from a given user account.
CAS shall maintain a mapping of security groups to specific system permissions and access rights.
CAS shall allow administrators to define and modify the set of permissions associated with each security group.
CAS shall enforce that a security group cannot be deleted if it is referenced by any existing permission mapping without administrator confirmation.
CAS shall validate that security group names are unique within the system before allowing creation or renaming.
CAS shall log all create, modify, and delete operations performed on security groups, including the identity of the administrator performing the action and a timestamp.
CAS shall provide the ability to query and retrieve a list of all security groups along with their associated permissions and assigned users.
CAS shall support hierarchical or nested security group structures, if configured, to allow inheritance of permissions from parent groups.
CAS shall restrict security group management functionality to administrators possessing the appropriate elevated privileges.
CAS shall provide validation to prevent circular references when nested security groups are configured.
CAS shall notify administrators of conflicts when a security group modification would remove permissions required by an active session or scheduled task.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall enable administrators to manage security groups.

Target allocation code: CAUI
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration User Interface

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAUI shall provide a screen listing all existing security groups with their names, descriptions, and member counts.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to create a new security group by specifying a unique group name and description.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to edit the name and description of an existing security group.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to delete a security group after confirming the action via a confirmation dialog.
The CAUI shall display a warning message when an administrator attempts to delete a security group that has active user assignments.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to add one or more users to a selected security group.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to remove one or more users from a selected security group.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to assign one or more permissions to a security group from a predefined list of available permissions.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to revoke previously assigned permissions from a security group.
The CAUI shall provide a search and filter capability for locating security groups by name or assigned permission.
The CAUI shall display the list of users currently assigned to a selected security group.
The CAUI shall display the list of permissions currently assigned to a selected security group.
The CAUI shall present validation error messages when an administrator attempts to create a security group with a duplicate name.
The CAUI shall require administrator role authentication before allowing access to security group management screens.
The CAUI shall provide a confirmation message upon successful creation, modification, or deletion of a security group.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to view an audit history of changes made to a security group, including who made the change and when.
The CAUI shall allow an administrator to export the list of security groups and their associated members and permissions to a file.
The CAUI shall prevent an administrator from removing all permissions from a security group that is designated as a default or system-required group.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to restrict environmental data publication based on source.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall provide a mechanism to associate environmental data records with their originating data source identifier.
QEDS shall maintain a configurable list of data sources that are permitted or restricted from publication.
QEDS shall evaluate the source of each environmental data record against the configured publication restriction rules prior to publishing.
QEDS shall prevent publication of environmental data records originating from sources marked as restricted.
QEDS shall allow publication restrictions to be defined at the individual source level.
QEDS shall allow publication restrictions to be defined at the source category or group level.
QEDS shall log all instances where environmental data publication was restricted due to source-based rules.
QEDS shall support updates to source-based publication restriction rules without requiring a system restart.
QEDS shall apply the most current source-based publication restriction rules at the time of each publication request.
QEDS shall provide an interface for retrieving the current source-based publication restriction status of a given data source.
QEDS shall reject publication requests for data whose source cannot be determined or verified.
QEDS shall notify authorized administrators when a source-based publication restriction rule blocks a data publication attempt.
QEDS shall support restriction rules based on source type, source identifier, or source ownership.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall log data transactions.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall log each data collection transaction, including source identifier, timestamp, and collection status.
Collector Services shall record the start time and end time of each data transaction it performs.
Collector Services shall log the outcome (success, failure, or partial success) of each data transaction.
Collector Services shall log the volume of data (e.g., number of records or bytes) processed in each transaction.
Collector Services shall log the identity of the external system or sensor source associated with each data transaction.
Collector Services shall assign a unique transaction identifier to each logged data transaction for traceability.
Collector Services shall log error codes and descriptive messages for any failed data transactions.
Collector Services shall log retry attempts made for failed data transactions, including the number of retries and their outcomes.
Collector Services shall timestamp all transaction log entries using a consistent system-wide time reference.
Collector Services shall store transaction logs in a persistent, non-volatile storage medium to prevent data loss.
Collector Services shall provide transaction log entries in a structured format suitable for retrieval and analysis by other subsystems.
Collector Services shall log any data validation checks performed during a transaction and their results.
Collector Services shall maintain transaction logs for a configurable retention period before archival or purge.
Collector Services shall log any communication interruptions or connectivity issues encountered during a data transaction.
Collector Services shall ensure that transaction log entries cannot be modified or deleted by unauthorized processes.

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide the capability to create, modify, and delete configuration records for environmental data collection sources.
CAS shall maintain administrative metadata describing system users, roles, and access permissions.
CAS shall provide an interface for authorized administrators to define and update system-wide operational parameters.
CAS shall validate configuration data submitted through the administration interface prior to persisting changes.
CAS shall maintain version history for all configuration changes to support rollback and auditing.
CAS shall enforce role-based access control for all configuration and administrative functions.
CAS shall provide mechanisms to activate, deactivate, or suspend configured data collection sources without system restart.
CAS shall log all configuration and administrative actions with timestamp and user identity for audit purposes.
CAS shall provide notification to relevant subsystems when configuration parameters affecting their operation are updated.
CAS shall support import and export of configuration data sets in a standardized format.
CAS shall provide the capability to define and manage scheduling parameters used by dependent services.
CAS shall provide status reporting on the health and availability of configured system components.
CAS shall support configuration of data quality thresholds and parameters used by downstream quality checking processes.
CAS shall provide administrative capability to reset or restore configuration settings to a known baseline state.
CAS shall restrict simultaneous editing of the same configuration record by multiple administrators to prevent data conflicts.

The Clarus system shall acquire, process, and disseminate environmental metadata.

Target allocation code: EMC
Target subsystem: Environmental Metadata Cache

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The EMC shall store environmental metadata received from the Environmental Metadata Services in a persistent cache.
The EMC shall provide read access to cached environmental metadata upon request from authorized subsystems.
The EMC shall maintain timestamps indicating the last update time for each cached environmental metadata record.
The EMC shall support the insertion of new environmental metadata records without disrupting concurrent read operations.
The EMC shall support the update of existing environmental metadata records while preserving data integrity.
The EMC shall enforce a configurable retention policy to purge environmental metadata records that exceed a defined age threshold.
The EMC shall detect and log duplicate environmental metadata entries prior to caching.
The EMC shall validate the structural integrity of incoming environmental metadata prior to storage.
The EMC shall provide a query interface to retrieve environmental metadata based on station identifier, sensor type, and time range.
The EMC shall maintain indexing structures to support efficient retrieval of environmental metadata by key attributes.
The EMC shall notify subscribed subsystems when new or updated environmental metadata becomes available in the cache.
The EMC shall support concurrent access from multiple subsystems without data corruption.
The EMC shall record an audit trail of all insert, update, and delete operations performed on cached environmental metadata.
The EMC shall provide a mechanism to export cached environmental metadata for backup purposes.
The EMC shall recover cached environmental metadata from the most recent backup following a system restart or failure.
The EMC shall limit total cache storage utilization to a configurable maximum capacity.
The EMC shall evict the least recently accessed environmental metadata records when the cache reaches its configured capacity limit.
The EMC shall report cache health status, including current size, hit rate, and error counts, to the Configuration & Administration Service.
The EMC shall reject environmental metadata entries that fail schema validation and log the associated error.
The EMC shall ensure that all cached environmental metadata is associated with a unique identifier to prevent ambiguous retrieval.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall acquire, process, and disseminate environmental metadata.

Target allocation code: EMS
Target subsystem: Environmental Metadata Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The EMS shall acquire environmental metadata from configured external data sources at scheduled intervals.
The EMS shall validate incoming environmental metadata against defined schema and format rules prior to processing.
The EMS shall normalize environmental metadata into a standard internal representation regardless of source format.
The EMS shall assign a unique identifier to each acquired environmental metadata record.
The EMS shall timestamp each environmental metadata record with the time of acquisition and, if available, the time of observation.
The EMS shall detect and flag duplicate environmental metadata records prior to storage or dissemination.
The EMS shall support acquisition of environmental metadata via multiple protocols, including but not limited to HTTP, FTP, and message queue interfaces.
The EMS shall log all environmental metadata acquisition attempts, including successes, failures, and error codes.
The EMS shall retry failed environmental metadata acquisition attempts according to a configurable retry policy.
The EMS shall transform environmental metadata into formats required by downstream subsystems prior to dissemination.
The EMS shall disseminate processed environmental metadata to subscribed consumers within a configurable maximum latency threshold.
The EMS shall provide an interface for querying environmental metadata by source, time range, and metadata type.
The EMS shall notify designated subsystems when new or updated environmental metadata becomes available.
The EMS shall maintain an audit trail of all modifications made to environmental metadata during processing.
The EMS shall reject and quarantine environmental metadata records that fail validation, along with the associated error reason.
The EMS shall support configurable mapping rules to translate source-specific metadata fields into standardized metadata fields.
The EMS shall provide status and health information regarding ongoing environmental metadata acquisition and processing operations.
The EMS shall enforce data retention policies for acquired and processed environmental metadata.
The EMS shall support concurrent processing of environmental metadata from multiple sources without data loss or corruption.
The EMS shall provide mechanisms to reprocess previously acquired environmental metadata upon request.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall manage environmental data and metadata according to the Clarus data sharing agreements.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide the capability to create, store, and maintain configuration records for each Clarus data sharing agreement, including participating organizations, data types covered, and access permissions.
CAS shall allow authorized administrators to define and modify data sharing agreement terms, including data usage restrictions, retention periods, and redistribution rights.
CAS shall maintain a repository of all active and historical data sharing agreements and make them available for audit and reporting purposes.
CAS shall enforce role-based access control to ensure that only authorized users can create, modify, or terminate data sharing agreements.
CAS shall provide the capability to associate specific environmental data sources and metadata elements with one or more data sharing agreements.
CAS shall validate that configuration changes to data sharing agreements do not conflict with existing agreements or system policies.
CAS shall provide notification mechanisms to alert administrators when a data sharing agreement is nearing expiration or requires renewal.
CAS shall maintain an audit trail of all changes made to data sharing agreement configurations, including timestamps and user identifiers.
CAS shall provide the capability to deactivate or terminate a data sharing agreement and propagate the resulting access restrictions to dependent Clarus subsystems.
CAS shall support the export of data sharing agreement configurations in a standardized format for review by external stakeholders.
CAS shall provide the capability to configure data retention and archival policies consistent with the terms of applicable data sharing agreements.
CAS shall verify that any subsystem requesting access to environmental data or metadata complies with the terms defined in the relevant data sharing agreement before granting configuration access.

The CAUI shall enable administrators to generate reports summarizing configured data sharing agreement settings for review and audit purposes.
The CAUI shall require administrator confirmation before submitting changes that affect data sharing agreement configurations.
The CAUI shall notify administrators when a data sharing agreement configuration is nearing expiration or requires renewal.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall collect environmental data from configured external data sources at scheduled intervals defined by the Schedule Service.
CS shall support collection of environmental data via multiple protocols, including but not limited to HTTP, FTP, and serial/RS-232 connections.
CS shall parse raw environmental data received from external sources into a standardized internal data format.
CS shall validate the structural integrity of collected data (e.g., completeness, format, checksum) prior to forwarding it for quality checking.
CS shall log the source, timestamp, and status of each data collection attempt.
CS shall retry data collection from a source a configurable number of times upon a failed collection attempt.
CS shall report collection failures, including source identifier and failure reason, to the Configuration & Administration Service.
CS shall forward successfully collected and structurally validated data to the Quality Checking Services for further processing.
CS shall support configuration of data source connection parameters (e.g., address, port, credentials, polling interval) via the Configuration & Administration Service.
CS shall associate each collected data record with metadata including source identifier, collection timestamp, and data type.
CS shall support concurrent collection of data from multiple independent data sources without data loss or corruption.
CS shall notify the Watchdog subsystem of any prolonged unresponsiveness or failure of a data collection process.
CS shall queue collected data for downstream processing when the Quality Checking Services are temporarily unavailable.
CS shall discard or quarantine data that fails structural validation, and shall log the reason for rejection.
CS shall support the addition, modification, and removal of data source configurations without requiring a system restart.
CS shall enforce configurable timeout limits for each data collection attempt to prevent indefinite blocking.
CS shall maintain a record of the last successful collection time for each configured data source.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall receive quality-checked environmental data records from the Quality Checking Services.
QEDS shall store qualified environmental data records in the Qualified Environmental Data repository.
QEDS shall associate each qualified environmental data record with its corresponding quality flags and metadata prior to storage.
QEDS shall provide an interface for retrieving qualified environmental data records by station, time range, and observation type.
QEDS shall disseminate qualified environmental data to authorized external systems and subscribers upon request.
QEDS shall support publishing qualified environmental data through standardized data exchange formats.
QEDS shall log all data dissemination transactions, including requestor identity, timestamp, and data scope.
QEDS shall enforce access control policies to restrict dissemination of qualified environmental data to authorized users and systems.
QEDS shall notify subscribed systems when new qualified environmental data becomes available.
QEDS shall retain qualified environmental data records in accordance with configured data retention policies.
QEDS shall provide mechanisms to archive qualified environmental data that exceeds the active retention period.
QEDS shall support querying qualified environmental data by geographic region and environmental parameter type.
QEDS shall validate data requests against defined access permissions before returning qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall provide status information indicating the availability and freshness of qualified environmental data for each monitored station.
QEDS shall support bulk export of qualified environmental data for specified time intervals and station groups.
QEDS shall detect and reject duplicate qualified environmental data records prior to storage.
QEDS shall provide an API for downstream systems to programmatically retrieve qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall maintain data integrity checks to verify that stored qualified environmental data has not been corrupted or altered.
QEDS shall support configurable data dissemination schedules for periodic distribution of qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall generate error notifications when qualified environmental data fails to be successfully disseminated to a subscriber.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall receive raw environmental data records from the Collector Services for quality assessment.
The QChS shall apply range checks to verify that each environmental data value falls within physically plausible sensor-specific limits.
The QChS shall apply step checks to detect implausible rapid changes between consecutive observations from the same sensor.
The QChS shall apply persistence checks to detect sensor values that remain unchanged for a duration exceeding a configurable threshold.
The QChS shall apply spatial consistency checks by comparing observations from nearby or co-located sensors.
The QChS shall apply temporal consistency checks by comparing current observations against historical trends for the same sensor.
The QChS shall assign a quality flag to each environmental data record indicating the outcome of the quality checks performed.
The QChS shall support configurable quality check thresholds retrievable from the Configuration & Administration Service.
The QChS shall retrieve environmental metadata required to contextualize quality checks from the Environmental Metadata Cache or Environmental Metadata Services.
The QChS shall record the specific quality check algorithm and version applied to each data record for traceability.
The QChS shall forward quality-checked data records, including assigned quality flags, to the Qualified Environmental Data Services.
The QChS shall log all quality check failures, including the failing check type and associated data record identifier.
The QChS shall support execution on a scheduled basis as directed by the Schedule Service.
The QChS shall provide status and health information to the Watchdog to indicate operational availability.
The QChS shall support reprocessing of previously quality-checked data upon receipt of updated quality check configuration parameters.
The QChS shall flag environmental data records as suspect, missing, or erroneous when they fail one or more applicable quality checks.
The QChS shall maintain configurable check enable/disable settings on a per-sensor-type basis.
The QChS shall process manually entered environmental data received via the Manual Entry User Interface using the same quality check procedures applied to automatically collected data.
The QChS shall generate error notifications when quality checking processing cannot complete due to missing or invalid input data.
The QChS shall preserve the original unmodified data value alongside any quality flag or annotation applied during quality checking.

The CAUI shall allow authorized users to deactivate or delete environmental data quality flag definitions that are no longer in use.

The CAUI shall present a list of all configured environmental data quality flags along with their associated status (active/inactive).
The CAUI shall restrict configuration of environmental data quality flags to users with appropriate administrative privileges.
The CAUI shall provide a search and filter capability for locating specific environmental data quality flag configurations.
The CAUI shall log and display the user identity and timestamp associated with the last modification of each environmental data quality flag configuration.
The CAUI shall provide a means for authorized users to export the current set of environmental data quality flag configurations.
The CAUI shall provide a means for authorized users to import environmental data quality flag configurations from an external file.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall provide environmental data quality flags.

Target allocation code: QEDC
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDC shall store a quality flag value for each environmental data element received from Quality Checking Services.
QEDC shall associate each quality flag with the corresponding sensor observation record, including sensor identifier, observation timestamp, and data value.
QEDC shall support storage of quality flag values conforming to a predefined enumerated set (e.g., Good, Suspect, Bad, Missing, Not Checked).
QEDC shall persist quality flags with sufficient precision to distinguish between multiple quality checks applied to the same data element.
QEDC shall retain a history of quality flag changes, including the previous flag value, new flag value, and timestamp of the change.
QEDC shall provide an interface for retrieval of quality flag values by data element identifier, time range, and station identifier.
QEDC shall ensure that quality flag data remains logically associated with its corresponding environmental data value even after data updates or corrections.
QEDC shall support querying of environmental data filtered by quality flag value.
QEDC shall maintain referential integrity between quality flags and the environmental data records they describe.
QEDC shall provide storage capacity to retain quality flag data for the full data retention period defined for environmental observations.
QEDC shall support the storage of multiple quality flags per data element when more than one quality check algorithm is applied.
QEDC shall ensure quality flag data is stored in a format retrievable by downstream services without requiring reprocessing of the original observation.
QEDC shall preserve the source identifier of the quality check process that generated each quality flag.
QEDC shall support data structures that allow null or unset quality flag values for data elements not yet evaluated.
QEDC shall provide mechanisms to export environmental data along with associated quality flags in a standardized data format.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall allow human quality checks of environmental data.

Target allocation code: CAUI
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration User Interface

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAUI shall provide a screen for authorized users to view environmental data records flagged for human quality review.
The CAUI shall allow authorized users to select a specific environmental data record and view its associated quality check history.
The CAUI shall provide controls allowing an authorized user to manually approve, reject, or flag an environmental data record following human quality review.
The CAUI shall allow authorized users to enter comments or annotations justifying a manual quality check decision on an environmental data record.
The CAUI shall display the current automated quality check status alongside each environmental data record presented for human review.
The CAUI shall provide filtering and sorting capabilities to allow users to locate environmental data records requiring human quality checks by station, sensor, time range, or quality flag.
The CAUI shall require user authentication and authorization before allowing modification of the quality status of any environmental data record.
The CAUI shall log the identity of the user, timestamp, and action taken whenever a manual quality check decision is submitted.
The CAUI shall provide a confirmation prompt before finalizing a manual override of an automated quality check result.
The CAUI shall display visual indicators distinguishing environmental data records that have passed automated checks, failed automated checks, or been manually reviewed.
The CAUI shall allow authorized users to revert a previously submitted manual quality check decision, subject to permission restrictions.
The CAUI shall provide summary reports or dashboards showing the number and status of environmental data records pending human quality review.

Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall provide a mechanism to flag environmental data records as pending human quality review.
The QChS shall allow an authorized human reviewer to view raw environmental data values alongside associated automated quality check results.
The QChS shall allow an authorized human reviewer to manually assign a quality flag to an environmental data record.
The QChS shall allow an authorized human reviewer to override an automated quality check result for a given environmental data record.
The QChS shall require a human reviewer to provide a justification comment when overriding an automated quality check result.
The QChS shall record the identity of the human reviewer who performed a manual quality check action.
The QChS shall record the timestamp of each manual quality check action performed by a human reviewer.
The QChS shall maintain an audit trail of all manual quality check actions, including prior and updated quality flag values.
The QChS shall support queuing of environmental data records that fail automated quality checks for subsequent human review.
The QChS shall allow a human reviewer to approve environmental data records that passed automated quality checks without further modification.
The QChS shall allow a human reviewer to reject an environmental data record and mark it as invalid.
The QChS shall allow a human reviewer to annotate an environmental data record with free-text notes describing the reason for a manual quality determination.
The QChS shall notify designated personnel when environmental data records require human quality review.
The QChS shall restrict manual quality check actions to users holding an authorized quality reviewer role.
The QChS shall provide the capability to batch review multiple environmental data records sharing common quality check failure characteristics.
The QChS shall support reversal of a previously applied manual quality flag by an authorized reviewer, subject to audit logging.
The QChS shall provide status indicators distinguishing records as automatically passed, automatically failed, pending human review, or human reviewed.
The QChS shall make the outcome of human quality checks available to downstream qualified environmental data services.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall provide a user interface for administration.

Target allocation code: CAUI
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration User Interface

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAUI shall provide a login screen requiring username and password authentication before granting access to administrative functions.
The CAUI shall provide role-based access control to restrict administrative functions based on user privilege level.
The CAUI shall provide a user management screen allowing administrators to create, edit, disable, and delete user accounts.
The CAUI shall provide a screen for configuring and managing system-wide parameters and settings.
The CAUI shall provide an interface for adding, editing, and removing collector site configurations.
The CAUI shall provide an interface for viewing and modifying schedule configurations for data collection tasks.
The CAUI shall provide a screen displaying system status and health indicators for administrators.
The CAUI shall provide an interface for viewing and exporting system audit logs and administrative activity history.
The CAUI shall provide a confirmation prompt before allowing an administrator to execute destructive configuration changes.
The CAUI shall provide input validation and error messaging for all administrative configuration forms.
The CAUI shall provide a session timeout mechanism that automatically logs out inactive administrative users.
The CAUI shall provide an interface for configuring quality checking rule parameters used by the system.
The CAUI shall provide a search and filter capability within administrative screens to locate configuration entries.
The CAUI shall provide an interface for backing up and restoring system configuration data.
The CAUI shall provide notifications to administrators regarding configuration errors or failed administrative operations.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to detect data submission errors.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall validate the syntax of each incoming data submission against the expected format prior to processing.
Collector Services shall detect and flag data submissions that are incomplete or truncated.
Collector Services shall detect data submissions that contain unrecognized or malformed data elements.
Collector Services shall verify that each received data submission originates from a recognized and authorized source.
Collector Services shall detect data submissions received outside of the expected transmission schedule or time window.
Collector Services shall detect duplicate data submissions received from the same source.
Collector Services shall verify checksums or other integrity indicators, when provided, to detect data corruption during transmission.
Collector Services shall detect submissions containing values outside of defined acceptable ranges for the applicable data type.
Collector Services shall log all detected data submission errors with a timestamp, source identifier, and error description.
Collector Services shall generate an error notification when a data submission fails validation.
Collector Services shall detect submissions that fail to include all required metadata fields.
Collector Services shall reject and quarantine data submissions that fail error detection checks, preventing them from being passed to downstream processing.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to access in-situ environmental observations from data collectors.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CS shall support connectivity to in-situ environmental data collectors via configured communication protocols (e.g., serial, TCP/IP, cellular).
The CS shall poll data collectors at configurable intervals to retrieve in-situ environmental observation data.
The CS shall support both push-based and pull-based data acquisition methods from data collectors.
The CS shall authenticate with data collectors prior to establishing a data retrieval session, where authentication is supported by the collector.
The CS shall parse raw data streams received from data collectors into a standardized internal format.
The CS shall timestamp each observation record with the time of receipt if a valid observation time is not provided by the collector.
The CS shall detect and log communication failures with data collectors, including connection timeouts and protocol errors.
The CS shall retry data retrieval from a data collector a configurable number of times upon communication failure before flagging the collector as unreachable.
The CS shall support acquisition of data from multiple concurrent data collector connections.
The CS shall queue retrieved observation data for downstream processing without data loss during transient system unavailability.
The CS shall validate the structural integrity of incoming data records prior to forwarding them for further processing.
The CS shall support configurable data collector connection profiles, including address, port, protocol, and polling frequency.
The CS shall log all data collection attempts, including successful and failed retrievals, with associated timestamps and collector identifiers.
The CS shall support the addition, modification, and removal of data collector connections without requiring a system restart.
The CS shall report data collector connectivity status to enable monitoring of collector availability.
The CS shall preserve the original raw observation data received from a collector for a configurable retention period prior to further processing.
The CS shall support acquisition of in-situ environmental observations in multiple vendor-specific data formats through configurable format adapters.
The CS shall associate each retrieved observation record with a unique collector identifier and location metadata.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to receive roadway weather measurements derived from VII data.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall provide an interface to receive roadway weather measurement data derived from VII (Vehicle Infrastructure Integration) sources.
The Collector Services shall support the ingestion of VII-derived roadway weather data in the format(s) specified by the VII data provider.
The Collector Services shall validate the structural integrity of incoming VII-derived roadway weather data prior to further processing.
The Collector Services shall parse VII-derived roadway weather measurement data to extract relevant environmental parameters (e.g., temperature, precipitation, pavement condition).
The Collector Services shall record the source identifier and timestamp associated with each received VII-derived roadway weather measurement.
The Collector Services shall log receipt of VII-derived roadway weather data, including data volume and reception time, for monitoring purposes.
The Collector Services shall detect and flag malformed or incomplete VII-derived roadway weather data records upon receipt.
The Collector Services shall convert received VII-derived roadway weather measurements into the Clarus internal data format for downstream processing.
The Collector Services shall forward successfully validated VII-derived roadway weather measurements to the Quality Checking Services for further processing.
The Collector Services shall support configurable connection parameters (e.g., endpoint address, protocol, polling interval) for receiving VII-derived roadway weather data.
The Collector Services shall handle communication failures with VII data sources by retrying data retrieval according to a configurable retry policy.
The Collector Services shall notify the Watchdog subsystem of persistent failures in receiving VII-derived roadway weather data.
The Collector Services shall maintain a record of the last successful data reception time for each VII data source.
The Collector Services shall support the reception of VII-derived roadway weather data over the network protocols specified for VII data exchange.
The Collector Services shall be able to receive VII-derived roadway weather measurements at a rate consistent with the expected VII data throughput.

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall poll external pavement sensor stations at configurable intervals to retrieve pavement-related observation data.
Collector Services shall support collection of pavement surface temperature observations from field sensor devices.
Collector Services shall support collection of pavement surface state observations (e.g., dry, wet, icy, snow-covered) from field sensor devices.
Collector Services shall support collection of subsurface pavement temperature observations where available from field sensor devices.
Collector Services shall support collection of chemical concentration or freeze point observations related to pavement treatment from field sensor devices.
Collector Services shall retrieve pavement observation data using multiple communication protocols supported by field equipment providers.
Collector Services shall timestamp each collected pavement observation with the time of collection.
Collector Services shall associate each collected pavement observation with a unique sensor or station identifier.
Collector Services shall detect and log communication failures encountered while attempting to collect pavement observations.
Collector Services shall retry collection attempts according to a configurable retry policy when a pavement data source is unreachable.
Collector Services shall normalize collected pavement observation data into a common internal format prior to forwarding.
Collector Services shall forward collected pavement observations to downstream processing components upon successful collection.
Collector Services shall support the addition of new pavement sensor data sources without requiring modification of existing collection logic.
Collector Services shall validate that incoming pavement observation data conforms to expected data types and value ranges prior to forwarding.
Collector Services shall maintain a record of the last successful collection time for each pavement data source.

The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from (roadway) vehicles.

Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall provide an interface to accept environmental data transmitted from roadway vehicles.
CS shall support ingestion of vehicle-sourced data using standard connected-vehicle data exchange protocols and formats.
CS shall validate the structural integrity of incoming vehicle data messages prior to further processing.
CS shall identify and record the source vehicle identifier associated with each incoming data submission.
CS shall timestamp each vehicle data submission upon receipt.
CS shall support receipt of vehicle data at variable and asynchronous reporting intervals.
CS shall queue incoming vehicle data for downstream processing without data loss under normal operating load.
CS shall detect and reject malformed or corrupted vehicle data messages.
CS shall log all accepted and rejected vehicle data submissions, including rejection reasons.
CS shall support concurrent data intake from multiple vehicle data sources simultaneously.
CS shall associate incoming vehicle environmental data with the geographic location provided by the vehicle at the time of observation.
CS shall forward accepted vehicle environmental data to downstream quality checking processes.
CS shall acknowledge receipt of vehicle data submissions to the originating vehicle data source when required by the interface protocol.
CS shall support configurable connection parameters for establishing communication sessions with vehicle data sources.
CS shall monitor the availability and health of vehicle data intake connections.
CS shall buffer incoming vehicle data during temporary downstream processing unavailability.
CS shall enforce authentication of vehicle data sources prior to accepting submitted data.
CS shall support secure transport of vehicle-sourced environmental data during intake.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from emergency vehicles.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall provide an interface to receive environmental data transmissions originating from emergency vehicle onboard sensor systems.
CS shall support ingestion of environmental data from emergency vehicles via multiple communication protocols, including cellular and wireless data links.
CS shall validate the structural format of incoming environmental data records from emergency vehicles prior to further processing.
CS shall extract vehicle identification information from emergency vehicle data transmissions for downstream traceability.
CS shall timestamp all incoming environmental data received from emergency vehicles upon receipt.
CS shall capture geolocation data associated with each environmental data reading received from emergency vehicles.
CS shall log the receipt of each environmental data transmission from emergency vehicles, including source identifier and receipt time.
CS shall detect and flag incomplete or malformed environmental data records received from emergency vehicles.
CS shall queue received emergency vehicle environmental data for handoff to downstream processing services.
CS shall support configurable data collection intervals for accepting environmental data from emergency vehicles.
CS shall handle intermittent connectivity from emergency vehicles by buffering data until successful transmission is confirmed.
CS shall acknowledge successful receipt of environmental data transmissions to the originating emergency vehicle system.
CS shall reject and log environmental data transmissions from emergency vehicles that fail authentication checks.
CS shall support the collection of environmental parameters including temperature, precipitation, visibility, and road surface conditions from emergency vehicle sensors.
CS shall maintain a record of the connection status for each emergency vehicle data source.
CS shall throttle incoming data rates from emergency vehicles to prevent collector overload during high-volume transmission periods.

- Implementation-independent: each LLR avoids unnecessary design choices.

- Uses requirement language: mandatory behavior uses "shall".

- Remove speculative, duplicate, unverifiable, ambiguous, or implementation-specific LLRs.
- Every returned allocation value must be exactly "CS".
- Preserve the HLR's intent and modal wording.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to receive weather data from weather service providers.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall establish connections to configured weather service provider endpoints to receive weather data.
The Collector Services shall support data retrieval from weather service providers via industry-standard protocols, including but not limited to HTTP, HTTPS, and FTP.
The Collector Services shall periodically poll weather service providers for new data based on a configurable collection schedule.
The Collector Services shall support receipt of weather data pushed from weather service providers via subscription or callback mechanisms, where supported by the provider.
The Collector Services shall authenticate with weather service providers using provider-specific credentials or tokens as required.
The Collector Services shall validate the format of incoming weather data against the expected provider schema prior to further processing.
The Collector Services shall log the receipt of weather data, including source provider identifier, timestamp, and data volume.
The Collector Services shall detect and handle communication failures with weather service providers, including retry with configurable backoff intervals.
The Collector Services shall queue received weather data for downstream processing without data loss during transient system unavailability.
The Collector Services shall support concurrent data collection from multiple weather service providers.
The Collector Services shall preserve the original raw format of received weather data prior to any transformation.
The Collector Services shall generate an error notification when weather data cannot be successfully received from a configured provider within a defined timeout period.
The Collector Services shall support configuration of connection parameters for each weather service provider, including endpoint address, port, and protocol type.
The Collector Services shall verify the integrity of received weather data using checksums or equivalent mechanisms, where provided by the weather service provider.
The Collector Services shall timestamp all received weather data with the time of receipt at the Collector Services.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system baseline data types shall be defined by the NTCIP ESS 1204 standard.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall parse and interpret incoming ESS data streams according to the NTCIP 1204 object definitions and data types.
Collector Services shall map raw ESS sensor values to the corresponding NTCIP 1204 defined data elements prior to forwarding for further processing.
Collector Services shall validate that collected data conforms to the data type constraints (e.g., integer ranges, enumerations, scaling factors) specified by the NTCIP 1204 standard.
Collector Services shall support collection of all essential ESS data objects defined in NTCIP 1204, including but not limited to atmospheric, pavement, and visibility measurements.
Collector Services shall reject or flag any incoming data that does not conform to the NTCIP 1204 defined data type structure.
Collector Services shall convert NTCIP 1204 compliant raw data values into the Clarus baseline internal representation without loss of precision or type fidelity.
Collector Services shall maintain a configurable mapping table linking NTCIP 1204 object identifiers to Clarus baseline data type definitions.
Collector Services shall support future updates to the NTCIP 1204 standard by allowing extensible data type mapping configurations.
Collector Services shall log any data type mismatches or non-conformances encountered during data collection for diagnostic purposes.
Collector Services shall ensure that units of measure for each collected data element align with the NTCIP 1204 specified units prior to data handoff.

The Clarus system baseline data types shall be defined by the NTCIP ESS 1204 standard.

Target allocation code: QEDC
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDC shall store qualified environmental data using data element definitions conformant to the NTCIP 1204 ESS standard.
QEDC shall represent each qualified environmental data record with the data type, units, and value range specified in the NTCIP 1204 standard for the corresponding ESS sensor category.
QEDC shall maintain a schema mapping table that associates each NTCIP 1204-defined data element with its corresponding qualified environmental data field.
QEDC shall reject or flag for correction any incoming data record whose data type does not conform to the NTCIP 1204 standard definitions.
QEDC shall preserve the NTCIP 1204 data element identifiers alongside the qualified values to support traceability to the standard.
QEDC shall support storage of all NTCIP 1204 baseline ESS data categories, including but not limited to temperature, precipitation, wind, pavement, and visibility measurements.
QEDC shall encode missing or not-applicable NTCIP 1204 data elements using the standard's defined error or missing-data indicators.
QEDC shall provide qualified data records to downstream services in a structure consistent with the NTCIP 1204 data element definitions.
QEDC shall version its internal data type definitions to track alignment with revisions of the NTCIP 1204 standard.
QEDC shall validate that units of measure for each stored data element match the units specified by the NTCIP 1204 standard prior to marking the record as qualified.

The Clarus system shall accept only observations that include location, timestamp, and source metadata.

Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall receive incoming observation submissions from configured data sources prior to any downstream processing.
Collector Services shall parse each incoming observation to extract location, timestamp, and source metadata fields.
Collector Services shall verify that a location field is present in each observation before accepting it for further processing.
Collector Services shall verify that a timestamp field is present in each observation before accepting it for further processing.
Collector Services shall verify that source metadata identifying the originating sensor or provider is present in each observation before accepting it for further processing.
Collector Services shall reject any observation missing location, timestamp, or source metadata and shall not forward it to downstream subsystems.
Collector Services shall generate a rejection notice, including the reason for rejection, for any observation that fails the location, timestamp, or source metadata completeness check.
Collector Services shall log all rejected observations, including the missing metadata fields, for auditing purposes.
Collector Services shall log all accepted observations, including confirmation that location, timestamp, and source metadata were present.
Collector Services shall validate that the timestamp field conforms to the system's required date-time format before accepting the observation.
Collector Services shall validate that the location field conforms to the system's required geospatial coordinate format before accepting the observation.
Collector Services shall validate that the source metadata field conforms to the system's required source identification format before accepting the observation.
Collector Services shall forward only observations that have passed the location, timestamp, and source metadata completeness and format checks to subsequent processing stages.
Collector Services shall apply the location, timestamp, and source metadata validation checks uniformly across all supported observation input channels.
Collector Services shall return an acknowledgment to the submitting source indicating whether the observation was accepted or rejected based on metadata completeness.

CS shall validate that each incoming observation includes a unit of measure identifier before accepting the observation.
CS shall reject any observation whose measurement type identifier does not match a recognized measurement type.
CS shall reject any observation whose unit of measure does not match a recognized unit associated with its measurement type.
CS shall maintain a reference list of accepted measurement types against which incoming observations are checked.
CS shall maintain a reference list of accepted units of measure associated with each accepted measurement type.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to acquire, process, and disseminate environmental data from across North America.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall establish and maintain network connections to external environmental data sources across North America using configured protocols (e.g., FTP, HTTP, TCP/IP sockets).
CS shall retrieve raw environmental data from external source systems at intervals defined by the Schedule Service.
CS shall support collection of data from multiple heterogeneous source formats, including but not limited to XML, CSV, and proprietary binary formats.
CS shall authenticate with external data providers using credentials and connection parameters obtained from the Configuration & Administration Service.
CS shall log the outcome (success, failure, timeout) of each data collection attempt, including timestamp and source identifier.
CS shall retry failed data collection attempts according to a configurable retry policy before marking a collection cycle as failed.
CS shall parse raw collected data into a standardized internal representation prior to forwarding for further processing.
CS shall forward collected and parsed environmental data to the Quality Checking Services for validation.
CS shall detect and report connectivity failures with external data sources to the Watchdog subsystem.
CS shall support dynamic addition, modification, and removal of data source connection configurations without requiring a system restart.
CS shall record metadata about each collected data record, including source identifier, collection timestamp, and data format version.
CS shall throttle or queue data collection requests to avoid exceeding rate limits imposed by external data providers.
CS shall notify the Configuration & Administration Service when a configured data source becomes permanently unreachable after exhausting retry attempts.
CS shall support collection of environmental data from geographically distributed sources spanning multiple time zones across North America.
CS shall provide status and health information about active data collection processes upon request from the Watchdog subsystem.
CS shall discard or quarantine malformed data that cannot be parsed into the standardized internal representation, and shall log the associated error.
CS shall support concurrent collection from multiple data sources without blocking or delaying collection from other sources.
CS shall apply source-specific data collection rules and parameters as defined in the Configuration & Administration Service.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to acquire, process, and disseminate environmental data from across North America.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall ingest raw environmental data records from Collector Services for qualification processing.
QEDS shall invoke Quality Checking Services to validate incoming environmental data records against defined quality control algorithms.
QEDS shall apply spatial and temporal consistency checks to environmental data prior to qualification.
QEDS shall associate quality flags with each environmental data element indicating pass, fail, or suspect status.
QEDS shall store qualified environmental data records into the Qualified Environmental Data repository.
QEDS shall retrieve environmental metadata from the Environmental Metadata Cache to support quality checking algorithms.
QEDS shall request updated environmental metadata from Environmental Metadata Services when cached metadata is insufficient or outdated.
QEDS shall reprocess environmental data records when updated quality control algorithms or metadata become available.
QEDS shall provide qualified environmental data to downstream dissemination interfaces upon request.
QEDS shall log the results of each quality checking pass, including timestamps and applied algorithm versions.
QEDS shall support configurable quality control thresholds as defined by Configuration & Administration Service.
QEDS shall detect and flag duplicate environmental data records prior to qualification.
QEDS shall notify Watchdog of processing failures or anomalies encountered during data qualification.
QEDS shall maintain an audit trail of all modifications made to environmental data during the qualification process.
QEDS shall support batch and near real-time qualification of environmental data records.
QEDS shall reject environmental data records that fail mandatory quality checks and route them to an error queue for review.
QEDS shall preserve original raw data values alongside qualified values to maintain data traceability.
QEDS shall support scheduled qualification runs as coordinated by the Schedule Service.
QEDS shall generate summary statistics on data qualification outcomes for administrative review.
QEDS shall ensure qualified environmental data conforms to standardized formats prior to storage.

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall expose a Clarus standard interface endpoint for accepting incoming environmental data submissions.
Collector Services shall validate that incoming data conforms to the Clarus standard interface data format prior to acceptance.
Collector Services shall support accepting data through multiple transport protocols compliant with the Clarus standard interface specification.
Collector Services shall reject and log any data submissions that do not conform to the Clarus standard interface schema.
Collector Services shall acknowledge successful receipt of data accepted through the Clarus standard interface to the submitting source.
Collector Services shall assign a unique identifier to each data submission accepted through the Clarus standard interface.
Collector Services shall timestamp all data accepted through the Clarus standard interface upon receipt.
Collector Services shall queue accepted data for downstream processing after acceptance through the Clarus standard interface.
Collector Services shall support configurable rate limiting for data submissions received through the Clarus standard interface.
Collector Services shall maintain a log of all data acceptance events occurring through the Clarus standard interface, including source identity and submission status.
Collector Services shall support authentication of data sources prior to accepting data through the Clarus standard interface.
Collector Services shall handle concurrent data submissions from multiple sources through the Clarus standard interface without data loss.
