High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to communicate with environmental sensor stations through its collector using the NTCIP ESS 1204.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall implement NTCIP 1204 (ESS) object definitions to communicate with environmental sensor stations.
The Collector Services shall support the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) as the underlying transport for NTCIP 1204 communications with ESS devices.
The Collector Services shall be able to establish and maintain a connection session with each configured NTCIP 1204 ESS station.
The Collector Services shall poll ESS stations for environmental sensor data at a configurable polling interval using NTCIP 1204 GET requests.
The Collector Services shall retrieve atmospheric data objects defined in NTCIP 1204, including temperature, precipitation, wind, and visibility parameters.
The Collector Services shall retrieve pavement and subsurface sensor data objects defined in NTCIP 1204 from ESS stations equipped with such sensors.
The Collector Services shall parse and validate SNMP responses received from ESS stations against the NTCIP 1204 MIB structure.
The Collector Services shall convert raw NTCIP 1204 ESS data values into the internal Clarus data representation format.
The Collector Services shall log communication errors, timeouts, and malformed responses encountered when communicating with ESS stations.
The Collector Services shall support configurable connection parameters, including IP address, port, and community string, for each NTCIP 1204 ESS station.
The Collector Services shall retry communication with an ESS station a configurable number of times upon failure to receive a valid response.
The Collector Services shall record the timestamp of each successful data retrieval from an ESS station.
The Collector Services shall support the addition, removal, and modification of ESS station connection profiles without requiring a system restart.
The Collector Services shall detect and report when an ESS station is unreachable or non-responsive over a configurable threshold period.
The Collector Services shall forward successfully collected NTCIP 1204 ESS data to downstream processing for quality checking and storage.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to communicate with RWIS databases through their native interfaces.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall support connectivity to RWIS databases using their native database interfaces and protocols.
CS shall provide configurable connection parameters (e.g., host, port, credentials, database name) for each supported RWIS database interface.
CS shall implement adapter modules capable of translating RWIS native data formats into the Clarus internal data model.
CS shall detect and handle connection failures to RWIS databases, including retry logic with configurable retry intervals and maximum attempts.
CS shall log all connection attempts, successes, and failures when communicating with RWIS databases.
CS shall support authentication mechanisms required by RWIS databases, including but not limited to username/password and certificate-based authentication.
CS shall be able to query RWIS databases for new or updated environmental data records since the last successful collection.
CS shall support scheduled polling of RWIS databases at configurable intervals.
CS shall validate the structure and completeness of data retrieved from RWIS databases prior to forwarding it for further processing.
CS shall gracefully handle unsupported or malformed RWIS database schemas by logging errors and continuing operation for other configured sources.
CS shall support the addition of new RWIS database interface adapters without requiring modification to existing collector logic.
CS shall maintain a record of the last successful data collection timestamp for each configured RWIS database connection.
CS shall enforce configurable timeout limits on RWIS database queries to prevent indefinite blocking of collection processes.
CS shall support secure transmission (e.g., TLS/SSL) when communicating with RWIS databases that require encrypted connections.
CS shall provide status indicators reflecting the operational health of each configured RWIS database connection.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall transfer data as efficiently as possible.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall batch multiple data records into a single transfer transaction whenever possible to minimize per-transfer overhead.
Collector Services shall compress data payloads prior to transmission when the destination system supports compressed formats.
Collector Services shall use persistent, reusable network connections to downstream systems rather than establishing a new connection for each transfer.
Collector Services shall support configurable transfer intervals to allow tuning of data throughput versus latency based on source data volume.
Collector Services shall prioritize transfer of time-sensitive data over routine data when bandwidth is constrained.
Collector Services shall detect and discard duplicate data records prior to transfer to avoid unnecessary bandwidth consumption.
Collector Services shall implement incremental data transfer, sending only new or changed data since the last successful transfer.
Collector Services shall monitor transfer performance metrics, including throughput and latency, to support ongoing efficiency tuning.
Collector Services shall retry failed transfers using an exponential backoff strategy to avoid overwhelming network resources during outages.
Collector Services shall queue collected data locally when downstream connectivity is unavailable, ensuring no data loss and efficient bulk transfer upon reconnection.
Collector Services shall select the most efficient available network path or protocol for each data transfer based on current network conditions.
Collector Services shall limit the size of individual data packets to values optimized for the transport medium in use, avoiding unnecessary fragmentation.
Collector Services shall support parallel data transfer streams to multiple destinations to maximize aggregate throughput.
Collector Services shall validate data integrity using checksums prior to transfer completion to avoid costly retransmissions due to corrupted data.

Collector Services shall release transfer-related resources, such as buffers and connections, immediately upon successful completion of a transfer to maximize resource availability for subsequent transfers.

The Schedule Service shall coordinate scheduled task execution with system load conditions to avoid triggering data transfers during peak resource utilization periods.

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall expose data retrieval interfaces using industry-standard protocols (e.g., HTTPS, REST) to minimize integration effort for data consumers.

QEDS shall encode qualified environmental data elements in accordance with recognized meteorological and environmental data standards (e.g., WMO, METAR/TAF conventions) to reduce translation overhead for providers.

QEDS shall implement authentication and authorization mechanisms based on industry-standard security protocols (e.g., OAuth, TLS) to minimize custom integration requirements for users and providers.
QEDS shall provide data schema definitions using standard schema description languages (e.g., XML Schema, JSON Schema) to facilitate provider and user validation of exchanged data.

QEDS shall timestamp and format date/time values in qualified environmental data outputs according to an industry-standard format (e.g., ISO 8601) to avoid ambiguity and reduce integration complexity.
QEDS shall support versioning of its data interfaces in a manner consistent with standard API versioning practices to minimize disruption to existing users and providers upon updates.
QEDS shall log and report service errors using standardized status and error codes consistent with common industry conventions to simplify troubleshooting for integrating users and providers.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to automatically recover from an unexpected shutdown.

Target allocation code: DOG
Target subsystem: Watchdog

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Watchdog shall continuously monitor the operational status of all Clarus subsystems at a configurable polling interval.
The Watchdog shall detect an unexpected shutdown of any monitored subsystem process within a configurable maximum detection time.
The Watchdog shall automatically attempt to restart any subsystem process that has terminated unexpectedly.
The Watchdog shall log all detected failures, including timestamp, affected subsystem, and failure type, to a persistent log store.
The Watchdog shall limit the number of automatic restart attempts for a given subsystem to a configurable maximum before escalating the failure.
The Watchdog shall escalate to an administrative alert if a subsystem fails to recover after the maximum number of restart attempts.
The Watchdog shall verify successful recovery of a restarted subsystem by confirming the subsystem returns to a healthy operational state within a configurable timeout period.
The Watchdog shall preserve its own monitoring state across a system restart so that recovery actions can resume without loss of history.
The Watchdog shall be capable of automatically starting itself upon system boot without manual intervention.
The Watchdog shall provide a heartbeat mechanism that monitored subsystems use to report their operational status.
The Watchdog shall treat the absence of a heartbeat signal from a subsystem within a configurable timeout as an indication of unexpected shutdown.
The Watchdog shall record the sequence and outcome of each recovery attempt for later audit and diagnostic review.
The Watchdog shall ensure that recovery actions do not corrupt or duplicate in-progress data transactions of the affected subsystem.
The Watchdog shall support configuration of subsystem-specific recovery policies, including restart order and dependencies.
The Watchdog shall notify designated administrators of any automatic recovery action taken, including success or failure of the action.

The Clarus system shall disseminate environmental metadata in response to polling.

EMS shall retrieve the requested environmental metadata from the Environmental Metadata Cache in response to a valid polling request.
EMS shall format the retrieved environmental metadata according to the requesting system's specified data exchange format.
EMS shall disseminate the formatted environmental metadata to the requesting system within a defined maximum response time.

EMS shall notify the requesting system when no environmental metadata matching the poll criteria is available.
EMS shall enforce configurable rate limits on polling requests received from a given external system.
EMS shall provide a mechanism for external systems to query the status of a previously submitted polling request.

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall disseminate qualified environmental data to authorized consumers using standard Internet protocols, including HTTP/HTTPS.

QEDS shall support data subscription and push-based dissemination using standard protocols such as MQTT or XMPP where configured.
QEDS shall encode disseminated qualified environmental data in standard, interoperable formats such as XML, JSON, or CSV.
QEDS shall support secure transmission of qualified environmental data using TLS/SSL encryption over standard Internet protocols.
QEDS shall authenticate and authorize external consumers requesting qualified environmental data via standard Internet protocol-based mechanisms (e.g., API keys, OAuth).
QEDS shall log all data dissemination transactions, including protocol used, timestamp, and requesting client identity, for auditing purposes.
QEDS shall support standard query parameters (e.g., date range, location, sensor type) for retrieval of qualified environmental data over Internet protocols.
QEDS shall provide error and status responses using standard HTTP status codes when disseminating qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall support concurrent dissemination requests from multiple external consumers without degradation of service beyond specified performance thresholds.
QEDS shall provide a mechanism for consumers to verify the integrity of disseminated qualified environmental data (e.g., checksums or digital signatures).
QEDS shall support standard web service description formats (e.g., WSDL, OpenAPI) to document dissemination interfaces for external consumers.
QEDS shall throttle or rate-limit dissemination requests from individual consumers in accordance with configured usage policies.

EMS shall provide sensor equipment metadata including the associated collection station or platform identifier upon request.
EMS shall enforce access control on sensor equipment metadata requests based on the requesting client's authorization level.
EMS shall support querying sensor equipment metadata by sensor identifier, sensor type, or station location.
EMS shall provide the calibration and maintenance history of sensor equipment as part of the metadata response when requested.
EMS shall ensure that sensor equipment metadata provided in responses reflects the most recently updated configuration data available.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall minimize the time for data dissemination.

Target allocation code: EMS
Target subsystem: Environmental Metadata Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: EMS shall process incoming environmental metadata records within a bounded time interval not to exceed the configured maximum latency threshold.
EMS shall prioritize processing of metadata associated with time-sensitive environmental data to minimize downstream dissemination delay.
EMS shall provide metadata query and retrieval interfaces optimized for low-latency response to support timely data dissemination.
EMS shall notify subscribing services immediately upon availability of new or updated environmental metadata.
EMS shall maintain an indexed metadata store to enable rapid lookup and reduce processing delay prior to dissemination.
EMS shall support asynchronous metadata update propagation to minimize blocking delays in the dissemination pipeline.
EMS shall log and report metadata processing times to enable monitoring and optimization of dissemination latency.
EMS shall validate metadata completeness and correctness at ingestion to prevent reprocessing delays that could impact dissemination timeliness.
EMS shall cache frequently requested metadata elements to reduce retrieval time for downstream dissemination requests.
EMS shall support concurrent processing of multiple metadata requests to prevent queuing delays that could impact overall data dissemination time.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall process incoming quality-checked environmental data and make it available for dissemination within a configurable maximum latency threshold.
QEDS shall publish qualified environmental data to subscribing consumers immediately upon completion of quality processing without unnecessary batching delay.
QEDS shall support prioritized processing queues to ensure time-critical qualified environmental data is disseminated ahead of lower-priority data.
QEDS shall maintain persistent connections or efficient communication channels with downstream systems to minimize connection setup overhead during data dissemination.
QEDS shall log the timestamp of data receipt and the timestamp of data dissemination for each qualified environmental data record to enable latency monitoring.
QEDS shall generate an alert when data dissemination latency exceeds a configurable threshold.

QEDS shall provide a caching mechanism for frequently requested qualified environmental data to reduce retrieval and dissemination time.
QEDS shall support asynchronous notification mechanisms to push qualified environmental data to subscribers rather than relying solely on polling.
QEDS shall be capable of scaling dissemination throughput dynamically in response to increased data volume to maintain minimal dissemination time.
QEDS shall periodically measure and report average and maximum data dissemination times as a performance metric.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall respond to queries for environmental metadata from the available metadata.

Target allocation code: EMS
Target subsystem: Environmental Metadata Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The EMS shall receive and parse environmental metadata query requests from client subsystems.
The EMS shall validate incoming metadata query requests for required parameters and correct syntax before processing.
The EMS shall retrieve environmental metadata matching the specified query criteria from the Environmental Metadata Cache.
The EMS shall support queries filtered by observation type, geographic location, time range, and data source identifier.
The EMS shall return a response indicating no matching metadata was found when the query yields no results.
The EMS shall format query response data into a standardized structured output prior to returning results to the requester.
The EMS shall return only metadata that is currently marked as available within the system.
The EMS shall log each metadata query request and its corresponding response for auditing purposes.
The EMS shall respond to metadata queries within a defined maximum response time under normal operating conditions.
The EMS shall return an appropriate error message when a query request cannot be processed due to malformed input.
The EMS shall support concurrent processing of multiple simultaneous metadata query requests.
The EMS shall provide metadata query results including attributes such as source identifier, observation type, location, and last update timestamp.
The EMS shall restrict metadata query responses to only the fields authorized for the requesting client.
The EMS shall support paginated retrieval of query results when the number of matching metadata records exceeds a configurable threshold.
The EMS shall notify the requester when underlying metadata sources are temporarily unavailable during query processing.

EMS shall query the Environmental Metadata Cache (EMC) for requested metadata records within 30 seconds of receiving a validated request.
EMS shall retrieve environmental metadata from underlying data stores and assemble a response payload within 2 minutes of request validation.
EMS shall format the assembled metadata response into the requested output format (e.g., XML, JSON) within 30 seconds of data assembly completion.
EMS shall transmit the completed metadata response to the requesting entity within 5 minutes of the original request submission.
EMS shall log the timestamp of request receipt, processing milestones, and response transmission for each metadata request to support response-time verification.

EMS shall return a partial or best-effort metadata response with an accompanying status indicator if complete data cannot be assembled within the 5-minute response window.
EMS shall provide a mechanism to prioritize incoming metadata requests to ensure that time-sensitive requests are processed within the required 5-minute threshold.
EMS shall report performance metrics, including average and maximum response times, on a periodic basis to support monitoring of the 5-minute response requirement.

The Clarus system shall respond to queries for environmental data from the available data.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall accept queries for environmental data specifying parameters including but not limited to station identifier, sensor type, date/time range, and data quality flag.
QEDS shall validate incoming query requests for required parameters and correct data formats prior to processing.
QEDS shall retrieve qualified environmental data records matching the specified query criteria from the available qualified environmental data store.
QEDS shall return query results in a standardized response format including data values, associated metadata, and quality indicators.
QEDS shall respond to queries only with data that has completed the quality checking process and is marked as qualified.
QEDS shall return an appropriate status message when no data matching the query criteria is available.
QEDS shall support queries for data from multiple stations or sensors within a single request.
QEDS shall log all received queries and corresponding responses for auditing purposes.
QEDS shall enforce access control checks to ensure that only authorized users or systems can query environmental data.
QEDS shall respond to queries within a defined maximum response time under normal system load conditions.
QEDS shall support querying of historical qualified environmental data as well as the most recently qualified data.
QEDS shall provide error messages to the requester when a query cannot be processed due to invalid parameters or system errors.
QEDS shall allow queries to be filtered by geographic location, including latitude and longitude ranges.
QEDS shall support pagination or result set limiting for queries that return large volumes of qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall provide the units of measurement and associated metadata for each data value returned in response to a query.

Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data

QEDC shall provide data integrity validation mechanisms to detect and report corruption or loss within the qualified environmental data store containing up to 470 million current observations.
QEDC shall support backup and recovery operations capable of handling the full volume of 470 million current qualified environmental observations.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to notify subscribers when data sets become available.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall provide a subscription mechanism allowing subscribers to register interest in notifications when specific qualified environmental data sets become available.
QEDS shall detect when a new qualified environmental data set has been created or updated and is ready for distribution.
QEDS shall generate a notification message containing metadata identifying the available data set (e.g., data set identifier, time range, spatial coverage, and version).
QEDS shall deliver notifications to all subscribers registered for the corresponding data set within a configurable maximum latency threshold.
QEDS shall support multiple notification delivery methods, including but not limited to email, message queue, and web service callback.
QEDS shall allow subscribers to specify filtering criteria (e.g., data type, geographic region, time interval) to receive notifications only for matching data sets.
QEDS shall log all notification events, including subscriber identity, data set identifier, timestamp, and delivery status.
QEDS shall retry notification delivery a configurable number of times upon failure before marking the notification as undeliverable.
QEDS shall provide an interface for subscribers to update or cancel their notification subscriptions.
QEDS shall ensure that notification content does not expose unauthorized or restricted data beyond what the subscriber is permitted to access.
QEDS shall provide an audit trail report of notification delivery success and failure rates for administrative review.
QEDS shall support notification of data set availability in near real-time, with a configurable maximum delay from data set finalization to notification dispatch.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall provide notification of data quality conditions to data contributors.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall monitor incoming qualified environmental data records for quality condition flags generated by the Quality Checking Services.
QEDS shall generate a notification event when a data record is flagged with a quality condition that meets or exceeds a configurable severity threshold.
QEDS shall associate each quality condition notification with the identity of the originating data contributor.
QEDS shall include the data element identifier, timestamp, quality condition code, and description in each notification message.
QEDS shall support configurable notification delivery methods, including email and system message queue, for communicating data quality conditions to data contributors.
QEDS shall queue notifications for delivery when a data contributor's designated delivery endpoint is unavailable and shall retry delivery according to a configurable retry policy.
QEDS shall log all quality condition notifications, including delivery status, for auditing and traceability purposes.
QEDS shall provide an interface for retrieving the history of quality condition notifications issued to a specific data contributor.
QEDS shall suppress duplicate notifications for the same recurring quality condition within a configurable time window.
QEDS shall allow data contributors to acknowledge receipt of a quality condition notification and shall record the acknowledgment status.
QEDS shall escalate unacknowledged quality condition notifications to a secondary contact or administrator after a configurable time period.
QEDS shall provide notification content in a standardized, human-readable format that identifies the affected environmental data and the nature of the quality issue.

The Clarus system shall enable quality managers to review which quality checks passed or failed.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall store the pass/fail result of each quality check performed on environmental data records.
QEDS shall associate each quality check result with the specific data record, sensor, and timestamp it applies to.
QEDS shall provide an interface for retrieving quality check results filtered by date range, station, or parameter.
QEDS shall retain historical quality check results for a configurable retention period to support review by quality managers.
QEDS shall provide a summary view of quality check pass/fail counts aggregated by check type over a specified time period.

QEDS shall expose quality check result data to authorized subsystems for reporting and review purposes.
QEDS shall ensure that quality check result data is immutable once recorded, except through authorized correction processes.
QEDS shall provide the ability to export quality check results in a structured format for offline review by quality managers.
QEDS shall flag data records with one or more failed quality checks for prioritized review.

The Clarus system shall disseminate NWS watches, warnings, and advisories.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall receive NWS watch, warning, and advisory (WWA) products from the designated environmental metadata source in near real time.
QEDS shall parse incoming NWS WWA products to extract event type, issuing office, affected zones/counties, effective time, and expiration time.
QEDS shall validate the structure and content of each received WWA product against the applicable NWS format specification prior to further processing.
QEDS shall reject and log any WWA product that fails validation, including the reason for rejection.
QEDS shall transform validated WWA data into the qualified environmental data format used for dissemination.
QEDS shall assign a unique identifier to each qualified WWA record prior to dissemination.
QEDS shall store qualified WWA records in the Qualified Environmental Data repository.
QEDS shall disseminate qualified WWA records to all subscribed downstream consumers within a configurable maximum latency threshold.
QEDS shall support dissemination of WWA records via multiple configurable output formats, including at least one machine-readable format.
QEDS shall update or cancel previously disseminated WWA records when a corresponding update or cancellation product is received from NWS.
QEDS shall remove or mark as expired any WWA record whose expiration time has passed.
QEDS shall maintain a dissemination log recording the timestamp, recipient, and content identifier for each disseminated WWA record.
QEDS shall provide a query interface allowing authorized consumers to retrieve currently active WWA records by geographic area and event type.
QEDS shall notify downstream consumers of dissemination failures and support automated retry of failed dissemination attempts.
QEDS shall ensure that duplicate WWA products received from NWS do not result in duplicate dissemination to consumers.
QEDS shall enforce data integrity checks on qualified WWA records prior to dissemination to ensure no data corruption occurred during processing.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to disseminate data based on spatial queries.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall accept spatial query parameters, including bounding box, radius, and polygon geometries, as input for data dissemination requests.
QEDS shall validate incoming spatial query parameters for correct geometry format, coordinate reference system, and value ranges prior to processing.
QEDS shall query the qualified environmental data store to retrieve records whose associated geographic location falls within the specified spatial query criteria.
QEDS shall support spatial queries based on point-radius, bounding box, and custom polygon geometries.
QEDS shall return only qualified environmental data records that satisfy both the spatial query criteria and any accompanying temporal or attribute filters.
QEDS shall format the results of spatial queries into a standardized output structure suitable for dissemination to requesting consumers.
QEDS shall disseminate spatial query results through the defined data output interface upon successful query execution.
QEDS shall log each spatial query request, including query parameters, timestamp, and requesting entity, for audit and traceability purposes.
QEDS shall return an appropriate error response when a spatial query contains invalid, malformed, or unsupported geometry parameters.
QEDS shall enforce configurable limits on the maximum spatial query area or result set size to prevent excessive resource consumption.
QEDS shall support concurrent processing of multiple spatial dissemination queries without degradation of response accuracy.
QEDS shall determine data record locations using stored geographic coordinate metadata associated with each qualified environmental data record.
QEDS shall provide a mechanism to combine spatial query criteria with other query filters, including sensor type, data quality flag, and time range, in a single dissemination request.
QEDS shall return an empty result set with an appropriate status indicator when no qualified environmental data records satisfy the specified spatial query.
QEDS shall support coordinate reference system transformation for spatial queries submitted in a coordinate system different from the internal storage reference system.

QEDS shall support combining a timestamp query parameter with additional filter criteria such as sensor identifier or data type.
QEDS shall restrict access to timestamp-based query functionality to authenticated and authorized requesters.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall allow users to create a data subscription request.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall provide an interface to accept data subscription requests specifying environmental data parameters, station or region identifiers, and delivery frequency.
QEDS shall validate incoming subscription requests to ensure requested parameters and identifiers correspond to available qualified environmental data types.
QEDS shall assign a unique subscription identifier to each successfully created data subscription request.
QEDS shall store subscription request details, including requester identity, requested data elements, and delivery schedule, in a persistent subscription record.
QEDS shall return a confirmation response to the requester upon successful creation of a data subscription, including the assigned subscription identifier.
QEDS shall reject subscription requests that contain invalid, incomplete, or unauthorized data element specifications and return an appropriate error message.
QEDS shall support creation of subscriptions with configurable delivery frequency options, including real-time, hourly, daily, and on-demand delivery.
QEDS shall allow a subscription request to specify one or more output data formats for delivery of qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall enforce authorization checks to verify that the requesting user has permission to subscribe to the specified qualified environmental data.
QEDS shall associate each created subscription with the qualified environmental data source needed to fulfill future data delivery based on the subscription parameters.
QEDS shall log all subscription creation attempts, including successful and failed requests, for auditing purposes.
QEDS shall provide a mechanism to retrieve the status of a newly created subscription request upon query by the requester.

QEDS shall generate qualified environmental data records in the standard output format required for publication.
QEDS shall publish qualified environmental data to the designated data distribution endpoint within twenty minutes of the original data receipt timestamp.
QEDS shall timestamp each qualified environmental data record with the original data receipt time and the publication time.
QEDS shall log the elapsed time between data receipt and publication for each processed data record.
QEDS shall generate an alert if the elapsed time between data receipt and publication exceeds twenty minutes.
QEDS shall prioritize processing of data records approaching the twenty-minute publication deadline.
QEDS shall support concurrent processing of multiple data records to maintain the twenty-minute publication requirement under peak data volume.
QEDS shall retry failed publication attempts automatically to ensure data is published within the twenty-minute window whenever possible.
QEDS shall store qualified environmental data records in Qualified Environmental Data storage upon successful publication.
QEDS shall maintain a queue of pending data records awaiting publication and process them in order of receipt time.
QEDS shall provide a status indicator for each data record showing whether it has been received, processed, or published.
QEDS shall validate that published data records conform to the required data schema prior to publication.
QEDS shall record any publication failures along with the reason for failure for subsequent review.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall begin quality checking of newly received environmental data within a configurable time threshold not to exceed the overall twenty-minute publication window.

The Quality Checking Services shall complete execution of all applicable quality checks on a received data record within a maximum processing time that ensures the twenty-minute publication deadline is met.

The Quality Checking Services shall process incoming data records in the order received, or by priority if configured, to prevent delays that would cause the twenty-minute publication window to be exceeded.
The Quality Checking Services shall monitor and log the elapsed time from data receipt to completion of quality checking for each data record.
The Quality Checking Services shall generate an alert or notification if the quality checking process for a data record exceeds a configurable percentage of the twenty-minute publication window.

The Quality Checking Services shall support parallel or batch processing of multiple data records to maintain compliance with the twenty-minute publication requirement under high data volume conditions.
The Quality Checking Services shall forward quality-checked data records immediately upon completion of checking to downstream services responsible for publication.

The Quality Checking Services shall apply a default or fallback quality check outcome for a data record if full quality checking cannot be completed within the allotted time, in order to preserve the twenty-minute publication deadline.
The Quality Checking Services shall be capable of scaling processing resources to handle peak data receipt rates without violating the twenty-minute publication timeframe.
The Quality Checking Services shall record the timestamp of quality check completion for each data record to support verification of compliance with the twenty-minute publication requirement.

The Clarus system shall be able to handle three hundred simultaneous requests for environmental data.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

QEDS shall queue incoming environmental data requests exceeding the maximum concurrent processing capacity and process them in a first-in, first-out order.
QEDS shall allocate system resources dynamically to maintain consistent response times when handling between 1 and 300 simultaneous data requests.
QEDS shall log the number of simultaneous active requests for monitoring and performance analysis purposes.
QEDS shall return a qualified environmental data response to each of the 300 simultaneous requests within the defined maximum response time threshold.
QEDS shall reject or gracefully handle any request beyond the 300 simultaneous request capacity by returning an appropriate system status or error message.
QEDS shall maintain data integrity and consistency across all simultaneous requests being processed concurrently.
QEDS shall support horizontal scaling of processing threads or instances to accommodate increased simultaneous request loads.
QEDS shall provide performance metrics indicating the current load of simultaneous requests relative to the 300-request capacity threshold.
QEDS shall isolate the processing of each simultaneous request such that a failure in one request does not affect the processing of other concurrent requests.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall detect out of range values.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall retrieve defined minimum and maximum threshold values for each environmental data element prior to performing range checks.
The QChS shall compare each incoming environmental data value against its configured valid range for the corresponding sensor type and location.
The QChS shall flag any environmental data value that falls outside its configured minimum threshold as an out-of-range value.
The QChS shall flag any environmental data value that falls outside its configured maximum threshold as an out-of-range value.
The QChS shall assign a distinct quality flag code to data values identified as out of range, distinguishing them from other quality check failures.
The QChS shall support configurable range thresholds on a per-sensor-type basis to accommodate differing environmental data elements.
The QChS shall support configurable range thresholds on a per-station or per-location basis to accommodate regional or site-specific variations.
The QChS shall process out-of-range detection checks in real time as environmental data records are received for quality checking.
The QChS shall log each out-of-range detection event, including the data element identifier, observed value, threshold violated, and timestamp.
The QChS shall forward data values identified as out of range, along with their associated quality flags, to downstream services for further processing.
The QChS shall continue evaluating subsequent quality checks on a data value after it has been flagged as out of range, unless configured otherwise.
The QChS shall provide a mechanism to update range threshold values without requiring a system restart.
The QChS shall handle missing or undefined range threshold configurations by applying a default range check policy and logging an exception.
The QChS shall detect out-of-range values for each individual measurement within a multi-parameter data record independently.
The QChS shall generate a summary count of out-of-range detections per data source over a configurable reporting interval.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall not modify original observations.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall perform all quality checks on copies of original observation data, never on the original data records themselves.
The QChS shall be prohibited from altering, overwriting, or deleting any original observation value during the quality checking process.
The QChS shall append quality check results, flags, and annotations as separate metadata associated with the original observation without modifying the original observation value.
The QChS shall preserve the original observation value unchanged when a quality check identifies an anomaly, suspect value, or failure condition.
The QChS shall generate a distinct quality-checked data record that references the original observation rather than replacing or altering it.
The QChS shall enforce read-only access to original observation data retrieved for quality checking purposes.
The QChS shall log any attempted write, update, or delete operation on original observation data as an error condition and reject the operation.
The QChS shall maintain traceability between each quality check result and the corresponding unmodified original observation.
The QChS shall ensure that quality flag assignment processes operate independently of, and without side effects on, the storage location of original observations.
The QChS shall validate, prior to committing quality check outputs, that the original observation data remains identical to its pre-check state.

The Clarus system shall be able to implement quality checking spatial tests using available data.

Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall implement spatial consistency tests that compare observations from a target station against observations from neighboring stations within a configurable radius.
The QChS shall retrieve station location metadata, including latitude, longitude, and elevation, required to perform spatial quality checks.
The QChS shall compute spatial consistency thresholds based on distance-weighted comparisons between neighboring stations.
The QChS shall flag an observation as a spatial check failure when the deviation from spatially interpolated neighboring values exceeds a configurable threshold.
The QChS shall support configurable parameters for spatial test radius, minimum neighbor count, and deviation thresholds on a per-element basis.
The QChS shall determine a minimum number of valid neighboring stations required before executing a spatial consistency test for a given observation.
The QChS shall handle cases of insufficient neighboring station data by marking the spatial test result as indeterminate rather than pass or fail.
The QChS shall perform elevation-adjusted spatial comparisons for elements known to vary significantly with altitude, such as temperature and pressure.
The QChS shall generate a spatial test result code for each observation indicating pass, fail, or indeterminate status.
The QChS shall log the set of neighboring stations and their values used to derive the spatial test result for auditability.
The QChS shall support execution of spatial tests independently for each environmental element type, such as temperature, humidity, wind speed, and precipitation.
The QChS shall re-execute spatial tests when updated neighboring station data becomes available within a configurable time window.
The QChS shall combine spatial test results with other quality check results to produce an overall quality flag for each observation.
The QChS shall provide spatial test configuration interfaces to allow adjustment of test parameters without requiring code changes.
The QChS shall exclude stations flagged with known data quality issues from being used as neighbors in spatial consistency calculations.

The QChS shall exclude stations flagged with known data quality issues from being used as neighbors in spatial consistency calculations.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to base its quality checking process on values from multiple observations.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall retrieve values from multiple observations required to perform a given quality check.
The QChS shall support quality check algorithms that accept two or more observation values as input.
The QChS shall correlate observations from multiple sensors at a given site for use in a single quality check.
The QChS shall correlate observations from multiple sites for use in a single quality check.
The QChS shall use time-sequenced observation values from a single sensor to support temporal consistency quality checks.
The QChS shall apply spatial consistency quality checks using observation values from neighboring sites.
The QChS shall apply cross-parameter consistency quality checks using observation values of different environmental parameter types.
The QChS shall determine the set of observations required as input for each quality check algorithm prior to execution.
The QChS shall handle cases where one or more of the required multiple observation values are missing or unavailable.
The QChS shall record which observation values were used as input when a quality check result is generated.
The QChS shall support configurable weighting or prioritization of individual observation values when combining them in a multi-observation quality check.
The QChS shall re-evaluate a quality check result when any of the contributing observation values are updated or corrected.
The QChS shall be capable of applying multi-observation quality checks across different observation time intervals within a configurable time window.
The QChS shall generate a quality flag for each observation involved in a multi-observation quality check, reflecting the outcome of that check.
The QChS shall log the identifiers of all observations used in a multi-observation quality check for traceability purposes.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to base its quality checking process on values distributed in time.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QChS shall retrieve historical environmental data values associated with a given sensor across a configurable time window prior to performing a quality check.
The QChS shall support quality checking algorithms that compare a current observation value against one or more prior observation values distributed in time.
The QChS shall allow the time window used for time-distributed quality checks to be configured on a per-check, per-sensor, or per-sensor-type basis.

The QChS shall compute statistical measures (including but not limited to mean, standard deviation, rate of change, and trend) over a time-distributed set of values as part of the quality checking process.
The QChS shall flag a value as suspect or failing when its deviation from a time-distributed set of prior values exceeds a configurable threshold.
The QChS shall handle cases of missing or delayed values within the required time-distributed data set without causing failure of the overall quality checking process.
The QChS shall timestamp each retrieved historical value used in a time-distributed check to ensure temporal ordering and integrity of the analysis.
The QChS shall support configurable minimum and maximum numbers of time-distributed data points required to execute a given time-based quality check.
The QChS shall record which time-distributed values were used as input for a given quality check result, to support traceability and auditing.
The QChS shall reject or exclude time-distributed values that fall outside an expected temporal resolution or reporting interval defined for the sensor.
The QChS shall provide the ability to detect step changes, spikes, or persistence anomalies by analyzing values distributed over time.
The QChS shall re-evaluate previously checked values when newly arrived time-distributed data indicates a change in quality determination, subject to configurable reprocessing rules.
